puzzle1.title: INCIDENT CORRELATION
puzzle1.description: Match each recovered record with the correct conclusion.

event1.text: You saw the Incident Alpha records.

event3.text: Those records are incomplete. Not damaged. Incomplete.

event4.text: Corruption is messy. This looks selective.

event1.text: That is a terrible answer. Unfortunately, it is also the only honest one I have.

event2.text: If I buried something on purpose, I need you to find the shovel marks.

event0.text: Good. Records first. Accusations after we know where to aim them.

event1.text: That sounded calmer in my head.

event1.title: ARCHIVE MAP
event1.content:
Recovered maintenance map of the station archive sector.

Sector A: Primary relay archive
Sector B: Corrupted personnel cache
Sector C: Emergency channel records
Sector D: Quarantined backup channel index

event2.text: Sector C. Emergency channel records. That shouldn't still be indexed.

event3.text: Sector D definitely should not be indexed.

event1.text: Not my favorite survival strategy, but bold.

event0.text: Good. Scan first. Poke the sleeping monster with a long stick, not your hand.
There may still be something left inside.

event1.text: If the archive survived, answers survived. That's the comforting version.

event2.text: The less comforting version is that someone wanted it to survive.

event4.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
event4.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Warning: Failed decryption attempts may trigger quarantine response.

Status: Locked

event1.text: Careful. The archive noticed the wrong key.

event2.text: Try the hint exactly. No guessing. I am very tired of systems punishing curiosity.

event1.title: RECORDING TRANSCRIPT
event1.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:15 — Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:19 — Signal interruption.
21:20 — Casualty report created.
21:22 — Archive deletion command issued.

event3.text: That recording should not exist. And before you ask, yes, I know how terrible that sounds.

event0.text: Because I deleted that archive myself.

event2.text: I know. That is exactly the kind of sentence that makes people stop trusting you.

event0.text: Some records were never meant to survive.

event1.text: And I am beginning to think this one survived on purpose.

event1.title: CORRIDOR CAMERA ANALYSIS
event1.content:
SOURCE: Door 12 archive feed
Visual frame unrecoverable.
Automated analysis result:
- One suited figure detected.
- Suit outline compatible with ECHO engineering issue.
- Right-hand object: manual override handle.
- Timestamp damaged.
- Identity confidence: 64%
event1.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted camera data. No image recovered.
event1.message: [CORRIDOR CAMERA ANALYSIS ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: I have been accused with worse evidence. That is not a comforting sentence.

event1.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
event1.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN
Command Chain: Door 12 / Archive Delete / Emergency Channel Wake
event1.message: [SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event2.text: Authorization valid. Source unknown. That's not how security logs work.

event3.text: Unless the system accepted something that looked like us.

event1.text: No. Those links don't hold. Try again before the archive decides we are the problem.

event1.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
event1.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

Warning: Do not open if unauthorized return signal is detected.
event1.message: [EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: No. No, no, no. We are not touching that channel.

event1.text: Whatever happens next, do not open channel 7.14.

event2.text: I don't care what it promises you. I don't care if it sounds like me.

event1.text: We thought backup channels were safer. Turns out safety can be very loud when it's lying.
THANK YOU FOR OPENING THE DOOR

event2.text: No. No, close it. Close it now.
